member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.BaseExporter`1.ForceFlush(System.Int32)
member.summary: Flushes the exporter, blocks the current thread until flush completed, shutdown signaled or timed out.
member.param: The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when flush succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown when the timeoutMilliseconds is smaller than -1.
member.remarks: This function guarantees thread-safety.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.BaseExporter`1.Shutdown(System.Int32)
member.summary: Attempts to shutdown the exporter, blocks the current thread until shutdown completed or timed out.
member.param: The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when shutdown succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown when the timeoutMilliseconds is smaller than -1.
member.remarks: This function guarantees thread-safety. Only the first call will win, subsequent calls will be no-op.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.BaseExporter`1.OnForceFlush(System.Int32)
member.summary: Called by ForceFlush. This function should block the current thread until flush completed, shutdown signaled or timed out.
member.param: The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when flush succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This function is called synchronously on the thread which called ForceFlush. This function should be thread-safe, and should not throw exceptions.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.BaseExporter`1.OnShutdown(System.Int32)
member.summary: Called by Shutdown. This function should block the current thread until shutdown completed or timed out.
member.param: The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when shutdown succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This function is called synchronously on the thread which made the first call to Shutdown. This function should not throw exceptions.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Internal.SelfDiagnostics.EnsureInitialized
member.summary: No member of SelfDiagnostics class is explicitly called when an EventSource class, say OpenTelemetryApiEventSource, is invoked to send an event. To trigger CLR to initialize static fields and static constructors of SelfDiagnostics, call EnsureInitialized method before any EventSource event is sent.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Internal.SelfDiagnosticsConfigParser.GetLogDirectoryRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
"LogDirectory"\\s*:\\s*"(?<LogDirectory>.*?)"
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase
Explanation:
○ Match '"'.
○ Match a character in the set [Ll].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Gg].
○ Match a character in the set [Dd].
○ Match a character in the set [Ii].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Cc].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Yy].
○ Match '"'.
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ Match ':'.
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ Match '"'.
○ "LogDirectory" capture group.
○ Match a character other than '\n' lazily any number of times.
○ Match '"'.

○ Match ':'.
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ "FileSize" capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.

member.name: T:OpenTelemetry.Internal.SelfDiagnosticsConfigRefresher
member.summary: SelfDiagnosticsConfigRefresher class checks a location for a configuration file and open a MemoryMappedFile of a configured size at the configured file path. The class provides a stream object with proper write position if the configuration file is present and valid. Otherwise, the stream object would be unavailable, nothing will be logged to any file.

member.name: F:OpenTelemetry.Internal.SelfDiagnosticsConfigRefresher.memoryMappedFileCache
member.summary: memoryMappedFileCache is a handle kept in thread-local storage as a cache to indicate whether the cached viewStream is created from the current m_memoryMappedFile.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Internal.SelfDiagnosticsConfigRefresher.TryGetLogStream(System.Int32,System.IO.Stream@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Try to get the log stream which is sought to the position where the next line of log should be written.
member.param: The number of bytes that need to be written.
When this method returns, contains the Stream object where `byteCount` of bytes can be written.
The number of bytes that is remaining until the end of the stream.
member.returns: Whether the logger should log in the stream.

member.name: T:OpenTelemetry.Internal.SelfDiagnosticsEventListener
member.summary: SelfDiagnosticsEventListener class enables the events from OpenTelemetry event sources and write the events to a local file in a circular way.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Internal.SelfDiagnosticsEventListener.EncodeInBuffer(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Byte[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Encode a string into the designated position in a buffer of bytes, which will be written as log. If isParameter is true, wrap "{}" around the string. The buffer should not be filled to full, leaving at least one byte empty space to fill a '\n' later. If the buffer cannot hold all characters, truncate the string and replace extra content with "...". The buffer is not guaranteed to be filled until the last byte due to variable encoding length of UTF-8, in order to prioritize speed over space.
member.param: The string to be encoded.
Whether the string is a parameter. If true, "{}" will be wrapped around the string.
The byte array to contain the resulting sequence of bytes.
The position at which to start writing the resulting sequence of bytes.
member.returns: The position of the buffer after the last byte of the resulting sequence.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Internal.SelfDiagnosticsEventListener.DateTimeGetBytes(System.DateTime,System.Byte[],System.Int32)
member.summary:
Write the datetime formatted string into bytes byte-array starting at byteIndex position.
[DateTimeKind.Utc] format: yyyy - MM - dd T HH : mm : ss . fffffff Z (i.e. 2020-12-09T10:20:50.4659412Z).
[DateTimeKind.Local] format: yyyy - MM - dd T HH : mm : ss . fffffff +|- HH : mm (i.e. 2020-12-09T10:20:50.4659412-08:00).
[DateTimeKind.Unspecified] format: yyyy - MM - dd T HH : mm : ss . fffffff (i.e. 2020-12-09T10:20:50.4659412).
member.remarks: The bytes array must be large enough to write 27-33 characters from the byteIndex starting position.
member.param: DateTime.
Array of bytes to write.
Starting index into bytes array.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Internal.SelfDiagnosticsEventListener.OnEventWritten(System.Diagnostics.Tracing.EventWrittenEventArgs)
member.summary: This method records the events from event sources to a local file, which is provided as a stream object by SelfDiagnosticsConfigRefresher class. The file size is bound to a upper limit. Once the write position reaches the end, it will be reset to the beginning of the file.
member.param: Data of the EventSource event.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Logs.OpenTelemetryLoggerOptions.IncludeFormattedMessage
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not formatted log message should be included on generated s. Default value: .
member.remarks: Note: When set to a formatted log message will not be included if a message template can be found. If a message template is not found, a formatted log message is always included.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Logs.OpenTelemetryLoggerOptions.IncludeScopes
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not log scopes should be included on generated s. Default value: .

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Logs.OpenTelemetryLoggerOptions.ParseStateValues
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not log state should be parsed into on generated s. Default value: .
member.remarks:
Notes:
As of OpenTelemetry v1.5 state parsing is handled automatically if the state logged implements or where T is KeyValuePair<string, object> than will be set regardless of the value of .
When is set to will always be . When is set to will always be set to the logged state to support legacy exporters which access directly. Exporters should NOT access directly because is NOT safe and may lead to exceptions or incorrect data especially when using batching. Exporters should use to safely access any data attached to log messages.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Logs.OpenTelemetryLoggerOptions.IncludeAttributes
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not attributes specified via log state should be included on generated s. Default value: .

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the for the current should be included on generated s. Default value: .

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Logs.LoggerProviderExtensions.ForceFlush(OpenTelemetry.Logs.LoggerProvider,System.Int32)
member.summary: Flushes all the processors registered under , blocks the current thread until flush completed, shutdown signaled or timed out.
member.param: instance on which ForceFlush will be called.
The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when force flush succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown when the timeoutMilliseconds is smaller than -1.
member.remarks: This function guarantees thread-safety.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Logs.LoggerProviderExtensions.Shutdown(OpenTelemetry.Logs.LoggerProvider,System.Int32)
member.summary: Attempts to shutdown the , blocks the current thread until shutdown completed or timed out.
member.param: instance on which Shutdown will be called.
The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when shutdown succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown when the timeoutMilliseconds is smaller than -1.
member.remarks: This function guarantees thread-safety. Only the first call will win, subsequent calls will be no-op.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Log record exporter.
The maximum queue size. After the size is reached data are dropped. The default value is 2048.
The delay interval in milliseconds between two consecutive exports. The default value is 5000.
How long the export can run before it is cancelled. The default value is 30000.
The maximum batch size of every export. It must be smaller or equal to maxQueueSize. The default value is 512.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MeterProviderBuilderExtensions.SetMaxMetricStreams(OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MeterProviderBuilder,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the maximum number of Metric streams supported by the MeterProvider. When no Views are configured, every instrument will result in one metric stream, so this control the numbers of instruments supported. When Views are configured, a single instrument can result in multiple metric streams, so this control the number of streams.
member.remarks: If an instrument is created, but disposed later, this will still be contributing to the limit. This may change in the future.
member.param: Maximum number of metric streams allowed.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MeterProviderBuilderExtensions.SetMaxMetricPointsPerMetricStream(OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MeterProviderBuilder,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the maximum number of MetricPoints allowed per metric stream. This limits the number of unique combinations of key/value pairs used for reporting measurements.
member.remarks: If a particular key/value pair combination is used at least once, it will contribute to the limit for the life of the process. This may change in the future. See: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-dotnet/issues/2360.
member.param: Maximum number of metric points allowed per metric stream.

remarks.para:
Notes:
The configured controls how measurements will be offered to s which are responsible for storing s on metrics.
The default provider configuration is .
Use or to enable s for all metrics managed by the provider.
If s are enabled on the provider by the configured then s will be configured on metrics using the defaults described in the specification: . To change the for a metric use the AddView API and .

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MeterProviderBuilderSdk.GetInstrumentNameRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^[a-z][a-z0-9-._/]{0,254}$
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match a character in the set [A-Za-z\u212A].
○ Match a character in the set [--9A-Z_a-z\u212A] atomically at most 254 times.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: T:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.Base2ExponentialBucketHistogram
member.summary: Represents an exponential bucket histogram with base = 2 ^ (2 ^ (-scale)). An exponential bucket histogram has infinite number of buckets, which are identified by Bucket[index] = ( base ^ index, base ^ (index + 1) ], where index is an integer.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The maximum number of buckets in each of the positive and negative ranges, not counting the special zero bucket. The default value is 160.
Maximum scale factor. The default value is 20.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.Base2ExponentialBucketHistogram.MapToIndex(System.Double)
member.summary: Maps a finite positive IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point number to Bucket[index] = ( base ^ index, base ^ (index + 1) ], where index is an integer.
member.param: The value to be bucketized. Must be a finite positive number.
member.returns: Returns the index of the bucket.

member.summary: Gets or sets the default histogram aggregation.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricReader.Collect(System.Int32)
member.summary: Attempts to collect the metrics, blocks the current thread until metrics collection completed, shutdown signaled or timed out. If there are asynchronous instruments involved, their callback functions will be triggered.
member.param: The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when metrics collection succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown when the timeoutMilliseconds is smaller than -1.
member.remarks: This function guarantees thread-safety. If multiple calls occurred simultaneously, they might get folded and result in less calls to the OnCollect callback for improved performance, as long as the semantic can be preserved.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricReader.Shutdown(System.Int32)
member.summary: Attempts to shutdown the processor, blocks the current thread until shutdown completed or timed out.
member.param: The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when shutdown succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown when the timeoutMilliseconds is smaller than -1.
member.remarks: This function guarantees thread-safety. Only the first call will win, subsequent calls will be no-op.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricReader.ProcessMetrics(OpenTelemetry.Batch{OpenTelemetry.Metrics.Metric}@,System.Int32)
member.summary: Processes a batch of metrics.
member.param: Batch of metrics to be processed.
The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when metrics processing succeeded; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricReader.OnCollect(System.Int32)
member.summary: Called by Collect. This function should block the current thread until metrics collection completed, shutdown signaled or timed out.
member.param: The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when metrics collection succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This function is called synchronously on the threads which called Collect. This function should not throw exceptions.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricReader.OnShutdown(System.Int32)
member.summary: Called by Shutdown. This function should block the current thread until shutdown completed or timed out.
member.param: The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when shutdown succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This function is called synchronously on the thread which made the first call to Shutdown. This function should not throw exceptions.

member.name: T:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricReaderTemporalityPreference
member.summary: Defines the behavior of a with respect to .

member.name: F:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricReaderTemporalityPreference.Cumulative
member.summary: All aggregations are performed using cumulative temporality.

member.name: F:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricReaderTemporalityPreference.Delta
member.summary: All measurements that are monotonic in nature are aggregated using delta temporality. Aggregations of non-monotonic measurements use cumulative temporality.

member.name: F:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricReaderTemporalityPreference.LowMemory
member.summary: Uses delta temporality for synchronous Counter and Histogram instruments and cumulative temporality for synchronous UpDownCounter, ObservableCounter and ObservableUpDownCounter instruments. This mode reduces SDK memory usage by avoiding the need to store both cumulative and delta states for temporality conversion.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Exporter instance to export Metrics to.

member.name: T:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.Base2ExponentialBucketHistogramConfiguration
member.summary: Stores configuration for a histogram metric stream with base-2 exponential bucket boundaries.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.Base2ExponentialBucketHistogramConfiguration.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.Base2ExponentialBucketHistogramConfiguration.MaxSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of buckets in each of the positive and negative ranges, not counting the special zero bucket.
member.remarks: The default value is 160. The minimum size is 2.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.Base2ExponentialBucketHistogramConfiguration.MaxScale
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum scale factor used to determine the resolution of bucket boundaries. The higher the scale the higher the resolution.
member.remarks: The default value is 20. The minimum size is -11. The maximum size is 20.

member.name: T:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.ExplicitBucketHistogramConfiguration
member.summary: Stores configuration for a histogram metric stream with explicit bucket boundaries.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.ExplicitBucketHistogramConfiguration.Boundaries
member.summary: Gets or sets the optional boundaries of the histogram metric stream.
member.remarks:
Requirements:
The array must be in ascending order with distinct values.
An empty array would result in no histogram buckets being calculated.
A null value would result in default bucket boundaries being used.
Note: A copy is made of the provided array.

member.name: T:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.HistogramConfiguration
member.summary: Stores configuration for a histogram MetricStream.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.HistogramConfiguration.RecordMinMax
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether Min, Max should be collected.

member.name: T:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricStreamConfiguration
member.summary: Stores configuration for a MetricStream.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricStreamConfiguration.Drop
member.summary: Gets the drop configuration.
member.remarks: Note: All metrics for the given instrument will be dropped (not collected).

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricStreamConfiguration.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the optional name of the metric stream.
member.remarks: Note: If not provided the instrument name will be used.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricStreamConfiguration.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets the optional description of the metric stream.
member.remarks: Note: If not provided the instrument description will be used.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricStreamConfiguration.TagKeys
member.summary: Gets or sets the optional tag keys to include in the metric stream.
member.remarks:
Notes:
If not provided, all the tags provided by the instrument while reporting measurements will be used for aggregation. If provided, only those tags in this list will be used for aggregation. Providing an empty array will result in a metric stream without any tags.
A copy is made of the provided array.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Metrics.MetricStreamConfiguration.CardinalityLimit
member.summary: Gets or sets a positive integer value defining the maximum number of data points allowed for the metric managed by the view.
member.remarks: Spec reference: Cardinality limits. Note: The cardinality limit determines the maximum number of unique dimension combinations for metrics. Metrics with zero dimensions and overflow metrics are treated specially and do not count against this limit. If not set the default MeterProvider cardinality limit of 2000 will apply.

member.name: T:OpenTelemetry.OpenTelemetryBuilderSdkExtensions
member.summary: Contains methods for extending the interface.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.OpenTelemetryBuilderSdkExtensions.ConfigureResource(OpenTelemetry.IOpenTelemetryBuilder,System.Action{OpenTelemetry.Resources.ResourceBuilder})

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with ability to delegate sampling decision to one of the inner samplers provided.
member.param: The to be called for root span/activity.
A to delegate sampling decision to in case of remote parent ( == true) with flag == true. Default: .
A to delegate sampling decision to in case of remote parent ( == true) with flag == false. Default: .
A to delegate sampling decision to in case of local parent ( == false) with flag == true. Default: .
A to delegate sampling decision to in case of local parent ( == false) with flag == false. Default: .

member.name: T:OpenTelemetry.Trace.SamplingDecision
member.summary: Enumeration to define sampling decision.

member.name: F:OpenTelemetry.Trace.SamplingDecision.Drop
member.summary: The activity will be created but not recorded. Activity.IsAllDataRequested will return false. Activity.Recorded will return false.

member.name: F:OpenTelemetry.Trace.SamplingDecision.RecordOnly
member.summary: The activity will be created and recorded, but sampling flag will not be set. Activity.IsAllDataRequested will return true. Activity.Recorded will return false.

member.name: F:OpenTelemetry.Trace.SamplingDecision.RecordAndSample
member.summary: The activity will be created, recorded, and sampling flag will be set. Activity.IsAllDataRequested will return true. Activity.Recorded will return true.

member.name: T:OpenTelemetry.Trace.SamplingParameters
member.summary: Sampling parameters passed to a for it to make a sampling decision.
Initializes a new instance of the struct.
member.param: Parent activity context. Typically taken from the wire.
Trace ID of a activity to be created.

The name (DisplayName) of the activity to be created. Note, that the name of the activity is settable. So this name can be changed later and Sampler implementation should assume that. Typical example of a name change is when representing incoming http request has a name of url path and then being updated with route name when routing complete.
The kind of the Activity to be created.
Initial set of Tags for the Activity being constructed.
Links associated with the activity.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Trace.SamplingParameters.ParentContext
member.summary: Gets the parent activity context.

member.summary: Gets the name to be given to the span/activity.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Trace.SamplingParameters.Kind
member.summary: Gets the kind of span/activity to be created.
member.remarks: For Activities created outside of ActivitySource, the Kind will be the default (Internal).

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Trace.SamplingParameters.Tags
member.summary: Gets the tags to be associated to the span/activity to be created. These are the tags provided at the time of Activity creation.

member.name: P:OpenTelemetry.Trace.SamplingParameters.Links
member.summary: Gets the links to be added to the activity to be created.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Trace.TracerProviderExtensions.ForceFlush(OpenTelemetry.Trace.TracerProvider,System.Int32)
member.summary: Flushes all the processors registered under TracerProviderSdk, blocks the current thread until flush completed, shutdown signaled or timed out.
member.param: TracerProviderSdk instance on which ForceFlush will be called.
The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when force flush succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown when the timeoutMilliseconds is smaller than -1.
member.remarks: This function guarantees thread-safety.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Trace.TracerProviderExtensions.Shutdown(OpenTelemetry.Trace.TracerProvider,System.Int32)
member.summary: Attempts to shutdown the TracerProviderSdk, blocks the current thread until shutdown completed or timed out.
member.param: TracerProviderSdk instance on which Shutdown will be called.
The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when shutdown succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown when the timeoutMilliseconds is smaller than -1.
member.remarks: This function guarantees thread-safety. Only the first call will win, subsequent calls will be no-op.

member.name: M:OpenTelemetry.Trace.TracerProviderSdk.OnShutdown(System.Int32)
member.summary: Called by Shutdown. This function should block the current thread until shutdown completed or timed out.
member.param: The number (non-negative) of milliseconds to wait, or Timeout.Infinite to wait indefinitely.
member.returns: Returns true when shutdown succeeded; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This function is called synchronously on the thread which made the first call to Shutdown. This function should not throw exceptions.
